# Security guidance

Do not file public issues for vulnerabilities.

If your report involves secrets, unsafe command execution, local privilege concerns, bypass behavior, exposed message contents, private workspace paths, or other sensitive details, stop here and follow `SECURITY.md`.

Use GitHub private vulnerability reporting for this repository if it is enabled.
If private vulnerability reporting is not available, contact the maintainer
privately through their GitHub profile before opening any public issue.

Use a public issue only for general, non-sensitive security questions such as documentation clarification or local hardening advice.

## Non-sensitive question


## Environment, if relevant

## Confirmation

- [ ] This issue does not disclose a vulnerability or private/sensitive information.
